methods.baseline: Household bus & coach fare spending is imputed from the LCFS in the PolicyEngine UK Enhanced FRS and calibrated to the DfT Annual Bus Statistics passenger-fare total (England, uplifted to the UK by population). Bus subsidy is the ETB-imputed government benefit-in-kind, calibrated to DfT net government support.
methods.allocation: Household fare is allocated to individuals by an age profile derived from the National Travel Survey (bus trips by age) adjusted for concessionary (free-pass) travel, so it tracks fares paid not trips. LCFS records fares at household level, so the per-person split is modelled, not observed.
methods.free_under_25: Fiscal cost = the bus fares allocated to people aged under 25, which the government would now meet. Equivalently a full subsidy for that group. Static (pre-behavioural).
methods.fare_cap_1pound: A £1 per-trip cap. The dataset records annual £ spend, not per-trip fares, so the cap is approximated as a fare reduction fraction applied to fares; a sensitivity grid spans 20-40%. Firming it up requires NTS trips-per-person (fares = trips x min(£1, avg_fare)).
methods.behaviour: All figures are static. Lower or zero fares induce extra trips (Scotland's under-22 free scheme saw large uptake); a behavioural trip elasticity would raise both ridership and cost.
methods.scope: Great Britain / UK (dataset is UK; fare data anchored to DfT England figures uplifted by population).

dft_fare.description: DfT Annual Bus Statistics, year ending March 2025, table BUS05aii: passenger fare receipts on local bus services (England), ~52% of £6.6bn operating revenue. Calibration target for bus_fare_spending, uplifted England->UK by population.

dft_subsidy.description: DfT Annual Bus Statistics, year ending March 2025, table BUS05bii: total net government support for local bus services (England), of which £0.8bn concessionary. Calibration target for bus_subsidy_spending, uplifted England->UK by population.

ons_population.description: ONS mid-2023 population estimates, used to uplift England-only DfT bus finance to a UK total. Indicative: bus use per head varies by nation.

nts_age_profile.description: National Travel Survey 2023 bus trips by age, concessionary-adjusted, used to allocate household fares to individuals.

scotland_u22.description: Transport Scotland's under-22 free bus scheme, a real-world comparator for a youth free-travel policy (and evidence of large induced demand).
